component_a_options.text: Evaluate the logical validity of this business argument: 'Successful companies invest in R&D. Our competitors invest heavily in R&D. Therefore, we should increase R&D spending to remain competitive.'
component_a_options.correct_answer: The argument commits an appeal to tradition/bandwagon fallacy. It doesn't establish that R&D spending causes success, only that successful companies do it.

component_b.text: Calculate the 5-year Net Present Value (NPV) at 8% discount rate for annual cash flows: Year 1: £45K, Year 2: £62K, Year 3: £78K, Year 4: £85K, Year 5: £90K. Initial investment: £200K.

component_a_options.text: This recipe uses archaic measurements: '3 gills of milk, 2 drachms of salt, 8 ounces flour'. Convert 'gill' and 'drachm' to modern units.
component_a_options.correct_answer: 1 gill ≈ 118ml, 1 drachm ≈ 1.77g. So: 354ml milk, 3.54g salt, 227g flour (8oz)

component_b.text: Scale the recipe from 4 servings to 17 servings, converting: 354ml milk, 3.54g salt, 227g flour.

component_a_options.text: What is the current UK ISA annual allowance limit for tax-free savings?

component_b.text: Compare two ISA options: Option A: 4.5% fixed for 5 years, Option B: 3.8% year 1, increasing by 0.3% each year. Which has higher returns on £15,000?

component_a_options.text: List the standard procurement approval steps for purchases over £10,000: budget check, vendor quotes, management approval, purchase order, delivery, invoice payment.
component_a_options.correct_answer: 1. Budget check, 2. Obtain 3 vendor quotes, 3. Management approval, 4. Issue purchase order, 5. Receive delivery, 6. Process invoice payment

component_b.text: Calculate total cost for 250 units: £18/unit for 1-99, £16.20/unit for 100-249, £14.40/unit for 250+. Shipping: £150 flat + £0.50/unit. VAT: 20%.

component_a_options.text: Your department head says 'We need to be more fiscally responsible this quarter' while rejecting your budget request. What is the likely implication?
component_a_options.correct_answer: Budget cuts or freezes are planned; non-essential spending will be scrutinized or denied; prioritize only critical requests.

component_b.text: Your Q4 budget is £48,000 with spending so far: Oct £16,200, Nov £14,800. With 3 major purchases pending (£6,500, £4,200, £8,100), will you exceed budget?
component_b.correct_answer: Spent: £31,000. Pending: £18,800. Total projected: £49,800. Exceeds budget by £1,800.
